concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in its left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in its right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Node Structure
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental building block of a tree, typically containing a value and pointers to its left and right children.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new value into a BST while maintaining its ordering property.

concepts.name: Search
concepts.short_definition: The process of locating a specific value within a BST by leveraging its ordering property.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing a value from a BST, which can involve several cases to maintain the BST property.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits nodes in ascending order of their values in a BST.

concepts.name: Pre-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits the root node first, then the left subtree, then the right subtree.

concepts.name: Post-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits the left subtree first, then the right subtree, then the root node.

concepts.name: Height
concepts.short_definition: The maximum number of edges from the root to the furthest leaf node in a BST.

concepts.name: Balance
concepts.short_definition: The property of a BST where the heights of the left and right subtrees of any node differ by at most one, affecting performance.
